summary: Started moving sysdolphin initialize code out of raw assembly by adding src/sysdolphin/initialize.c to the build and removing the first three functions from asm/initialize.s. HSD_InitComponent was carried over as inline assembly in C, while HSD_GXSetFifoObj and the empty HSD_DVDInit were represented as C. The PR also introduced the HSD_MemReport struct, a stub leak.h, and HSD_VI_XFB_MAX in video.h to support declarations used by initialize.c.
what_changed: Added new source file src/sysdolphin/initialize.c containing HSD_InitComponent, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, and HSD_DVDInit.
Removed the corresponding HSD_InitComponent, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, and HSD_DVDInit assembly blocks from asm/initialize.s.

Registered $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/initialize.o in TEXT_O_FILES before asm/initialize.o so the new C object participates in the build while remaining assembly continues to link from asm/initialize.o.

Kept HSD_InitComponent as an asm function inside initialize.c using nofralloc and the original instruction sequence, while adjusting small-data references to C-visible extern labels such as lbl_804D5E04(r13), lbl_804DE590(r2), and lbl_804D76CC(r13).
Implemented HSD_GXSetFifoObj in C, assigning lbl_804C0954.gxfifo from lbl_804D5E0C and storing the passed fifo pointer into lbl_804D76C4.
Implemented HSD_DVDInit as an empty C function returning immediately.
Added HSD_MemReport to initialize.h with fields total, system, xfb, gxfifo, and heap.
Added HSD_VI_XFB_MAX 3 to video.h for the framebuffer extern declaration.
Added an empty guarded src/sysdolphin/leak.h header, likely as a placeholder include needed by initialize.c.
smart_moves: Split only the first three functions from initialize.s instead of attempting the whole file, preserving the remaining assembly object and reducing matching risk.
Used inline assembly for the large HSD_InitComponent body, allowing the function to move into the C translation unit without first solving all high-level C matching issues.
Converted the simple HSD_GXSetFifoObj and no-op HSD_DVDInit to C immediately, extracting low-risk functions while leaving complex initialization code as assembly.
Introduced the minimal HSD_MemReport type needed to express the memReport global access in C rather than treating the structure as an untyped blob.
Added initialize.o before asm/initialize.o in obj_files.mk, matching the pattern for partial asm-to-C migration where a C object replaces only some symbols from an asm file.
decomp_lessons: For early decompilation of a file, it is acceptable to migrate a subset of functions into a new C file and keep the rest in the existing asm file.
Simple setter functions are good candidates for first C matches because their global accesses can reveal useful struct field names and small-data globals.
A no-op assembly function can often be safely represented as an empty C function when the generated return sequence matches.
When moving assembly into a C file, extern declarations for linker labels may be necessary before the corresponding globals have meaningful names.
Small header additions such as constants and struct shells can unblock C compilation while preserving later opportunities for more accurate typing.
naming_conventions: Public sysdolphin functions use the HSD_ prefix, as in HSD_InitComponent, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, and HSD_DVDInit.
Struct typedefs use an underscored tag plus typedef form, shown by struct _HSD_MemReport typedefed to HSD_MemReport.

Temporary unknown globals retain linker-label names such as lbl_804C0948, lbl_804C0954, lbl_804D5E04, lbl_804D5E0C, lbl_804D76C4, and lbl_804D76CC, with comments documenting suspected roles like FrameBuffer, memReport, rmode, iparam_fifo_size, DefaultFifoObj, and init_done.
Video-related constants use HSD_VI_ uppercase macro naming, shown by HSD_VI_XFB_MAX.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved original assembly into an asm void function with nofralloc to preserve the exact prologue/stack handling for HSD_InitComponent.
Used C extern declarations for global labels referenced from inline assembly and C code.
Used #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around HSD_GXSetFifoObj, indicating compiler codegen control was needed or expected for matching.
Represented HSD_DVDInit as an empty C function to match the original single blr body.
Kept the remaining functions in asm/initialize.s after HSD_DVDInit, beginning with HSD_AllocateXFB, avoiding a full-file migration.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace the inline assembly HSD_InitComponent with high-level C.
Find later PRs that rename lbl_804C0954, lbl_804D5E0C, lbl_804D76C4, or lbl_804D76CC to meaningful sysdolphin globals.
Find later PRs that flesh out leak.h or move HSD_MemReport to a more appropriate header.
Find later changes to initialize.c around HSD_AllocateXFB or other remaining functions from asm/initialize.s.
